decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of small, low-cost white creatures and pump spells. By utilizing cards like Thraben Inspector, Militia Bugler, and Novice Inspector to quickly build a board presence, followed by cards like Guardians' Pledge and Battle Screech to boost their power, this deck aims to deliver consistent damage early in the game. The inclusion of card draw and mana fixing with cards like Raffine's Informant and Boros Garrison helps ensure a steady stream of threats to put pressure on the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle using Elf creatures. By utilizing cards like Lead the Stampede and Winding Way, the deck aims to quickly build a board presence and generate card advantage. The key strategy is to ramp up mana with Llanowar Elves and Priest of Titania, while also utilizing cards like Timberwatch Elf and Nyxborn Hydra to deal massive damage. The deck also includes support cards like Quirion Ranger and Masked Vandal to control the battlefield and disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly flood the board with small creatures that can be buffed with powerful auras. By playing efficient threats like Slippery Bogle and Gladecover Scout, and then boosting them with cards like Rancor, Ethereal Armor, and Ancestral Mask, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. The use of multiple enchantment-based ramp spells, like Utopia Sprawl and Abundant Growth, helps ensure that the deck can consistently get its threats on the board quickly and maintain pressure throughout the game.

This deck focuses on creating a strong defense with walls while also generating mana with creatures like Axebane Guardian and Overgrown Battlement. The goal is to assemble the Freed from the Real combo by enchanting a creature like Galvanic Alchemist to generate infinite mana. This can then be used to cast Valakut Invoker or Reaping the Graves to win the game. The deck also includes card draw and creature search to help assemble the combo pieces efficiently.

The key concept of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By utilizing cards like Brainstorm, Thought Scour, and Deep Analysis, the deck aims to keep the opponent's threats in check while also digging for key cards. Gurmag Angler provides a strong finisher, while cards like Tolarian Terror and Snuff Out help deal with opposing creatures. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Unexpected Fangs and Suffocating Fumes to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. In essence, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent through careful resource management and efficient spellcasting.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game using cheap and efficient spells and creatures. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy by countering their spells, drawing cards, and removing their threats using removal spells like Snuff Out and Cast Down. The deck also utilizes cards like Mental Note and Thought Scour to fill up the graveyard quickly, allowing for the early casting of Gurmag Angler. This Dimir Control deck focuses on outlasting opponents through resource management and strategic decision-making.

The main focus of this Aggro - Burn deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using low-cost creatures like Voldaren Epicure and Sneaky Snacker, as well as direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast. The deck also includes cards like Fiery Temper and Lava Dart to efficiently deal damage while maintaining a fast pace. With cards like Highway Robbery and Grab the Prize for additional reach, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a relentless assault of burn spells and creature attacks.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive white weenie strategy. By quickly building a board of low-cost creatures like Lunarch Veteran and Thraben Inspector, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. Cards like Guardians' Pledge and Battle Screech can provide additional burst damage, while cards like Prismatic Strands and Militia Bugler offer utility and card advantage. The deck also includes some bounce effects with cards like Kor Skyfisher to protect key creatures and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this Aggro - Elves deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of elves, utilizing cards like Priest of Titania and Elvish Mystic to generate mana for big plays. Generous Ent and Timberwatch Elf provide powerful boosts to the team, while Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help refill the hand with more elves. The deck aims to establish a strong board presence early on and maintain pressure throughout the game to secure a victory.

The main concept of this Rakdos Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of low-cost, aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By utilizing cards like Alms of the Vein and Fiery Temper, the deck aims to quickly chip away at the opponent's life total while establishing board presence with creatures such as Voldaren Epicure and Sneaky Snacker. The deck also includes some recursion with Faithless Looting to draw into more threats and removal spells like Lightning Bolt to control the board and maintain pressure on the opponent.

This Aggro Jund deck focuses on quickly overwhelming your opponent with aggressive creatures and value from sacrificing artifacts. By utilizing cards like Refurbished Familiar, Writhing Chrysalis, and Cleansing Wildfire, you can quickly establish a board presence while disrupting your opponent's game plan with removal spells like Cast Down and Terminate. Additionally, the deck has graveyard hate with Nihil Spellbomb and disruption with Bojuka Bog. The deck aims to be fast and disruptive, constantly pressuring your opponent while disrupting their strategy.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage. It aims to control the pace of the game by using efficient creatures like Augur of Bolas and Spellstutter Sprite, while also generating value with cards like Ninja of the Deep Hours and Thorn of the Black Rose. The deck also includes disruption tools like Brainstorm and Dispel, as well as removal spells such as Cast Down and Snuff Out. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements while also providing some utility lands for additional advantages.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive strategy. By leveraging cards like Galvanic Blast, Myr Enforcer, and Great Furnace, the deck aims to deal damage efficiently and consistently. The Affinity archetype allows for explosive starts and powerful plays by reducing the costs of artifacts and enabling strong synergies between cards. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and use its artifacts to control the battlefield while attacking aggressively to secure victories.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by countering their spells and controlling the board with the use of bounce effects. This is achieved through a combination of card draw spells and efficient counterspells to maintain card advantage and tempo. The win condition is to play large threats like Cryptic Serpent or Sleep of the Dead to close out the game once the opponent's resources are depleted. The deck aims to control the flow of the game and outvalue the opponent through careful decision making and resource management.

The central idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and burn spells. By efficiently dealing damage with cards like Lightning Bolt and Fiery Temper, while also generating card advantage with Grab the Prize and Faithless Looting, the deck aims to keep up relentless pressure on the opponent. Cards like Guttersnipe and Highway Robbery further increase the damage output, while creatures like Kessig Flamebreather and Voldaren Epicure provide an additional threat on the battlefield. Fireblast serves as a powerful finisher to close out games quickly.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent's life total by utilizing low-cost burn spells and creature abilities. By efficiently managing resources and maintaining pressure with aggressive creatures like Guttersnipe and Kessig Flamebreather, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. Combining direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast with creature damage and card draw from cards like Grab the Prize and Sneaky Snacker, the deck seeks to finish off the opponent quickly with a high tempo aggro strategy.
